title: Pronouncing Pronouns and Contractions
learningGoal: Accurately pronounce personal pronouns and the natural contracted forms of 'be' verbs.
instructions: 代名詞とbe動詞、そしてネイティブがよく使う「短縮形」の発音練習です。音声を聞いて、マイクに向かってリピートしてください。(Practice pronouncing pronouns, 'be' verbs, and their contracted forms. Listen to the audio and repeat into the microphone.)

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Pronounce both words clearly: ai am.
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Saying 'ai amu' with a heavy 'u' sound at the end.

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Close your lips at the end for the 'm' sound.

targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Saying 'yu aa' without the English 'r' sound.

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Sounds very similar to 'your'. Pull your tongue back for the 'r'.
Make it one syllable with a buzzing 'z' sound at the end.
The 'sh' sound is sharp, like telling someone to be quiet.
Put your tongue between your teeth for 'They'.
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Saying 'Zei aa' like the Japanese 'za zi zu ze zo'.

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Sounds exactly like 'their' and 'there'. Tongue between teeth, then pull back for 'r'.
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Saying 'Zea' without the TH sound or the R sound.

expectedPronunciationText: I am I'm You are You're He is He's She is She's They are They're

feedbackRules.onPass: Great job! You pronounced the contractions very naturally.
feedbackRules.onFail: Keep practicing! Don't add extra vowel sounds at the end of words (e.g. say 'I'm', not 'I-mu').
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort! Make sure you are using your teeth for the 'TH' in 'They'.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.exactCorrectTranscript: I am I'm You are You're He is He's She is She's They are They're
acceptedVariants: i am i'm you are you're he is he's she is she's they are they're
adminCorrectAnswerSet.nativeScriptReference: I am I'm You are You're He is He's She is She's They are They're
adminCorrectAnswerSet.transliteration: ai am aim yu ar yor hi iz hiz shi iz shiz they ar ther

audioAssets.audioTranscript: I am, I'm, You are, You're, He is, He's, She is, She's, They are, They're

hintText: In spoken English, native speakers almost always use the short forms (I'm, You're, He's).
